metadata.title: Build the Weekly Operations Packet with Claude: Capstone Demo

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The conflict demo is what I want to reproduce: status.md said 'approved' and email_digest.md said 'pending' for the same decision, and only the source tags let the CONFLICTING SOURCES flag pause assembly. I'll give you two of my own source files that disagree. Please (1) assemble a status section with a source tag after every data point, (2) surface any item where two sources conflict with both tagged values shown, and (3) tell me which source I should treat as authoritative and the reasoning I'd use to resolve it.

props.command: The conflict demo is what I want to reproduce: status.md said 'approved' and email_digest.md said 'pending' for the same decision, and only the source tags let the CONFLICTING SOURCES flag pause assembly. I'll give you two of my own source files that disagree. Please (1) assemble a status section with a source tag after every data point, (2) surface any item where two sources conflict with both tagged values shown, and (3) tell me which source I should treat as authoritative and the reasoning I'd use to resolve it.
props.segment: Build the Weekly Operations Packet with

beats.narration_text: Source traceability is the difference between a packet you can act on and a packet you have to re-verify.

The weekly ops packet fails as a Claude Code task — fully autonomous — because the source documents change weekly: different formats, different structures, different gaps. CoWork tier is correct: the assembly runs with Claude, but the human reviews source conflicts before the packet is finalized.

Assemble the status section of the weekly operations packet from this project status file. Include project name, current phase, key decisions since last week with dates, blockers, and next actions. Add a source tag after each data point.

props.command: claude "Assemble the status section of the weekly operations packet from this project status file. Include: project name, current phase, key decisions since last week (with dates), blockers, and next actions. Add a source: tag after each data point." < status.md

beats.narration_text: The ops packet script assembles from 4 source documents, runs each section through a separate Claude pass, adds source traceability tags, and produces an audit note.

Packet assembled: 4 sections, 23 data points, all with source: filename and section tags. Source index: status.md contributed 12 points, metrics.csv 7 points, calendar.ics 3 points, email_digest.md 1 point. Every claim is traceable. Packet is under 2 pages.
Introduce a conflict: status.md says approved for a decision; email_digest.md says pending for the same decision.

CONFLICTING SOURCES flag appears for the Decision item: status.md says approved; email_digest.md says pending. Assembly pauses. Human reviews both sources, determines which is authoritative, manually resolves. The conflict surfaced because both sources were tagged — without traceability, the model would have silently chosen one.

Source traceability is not metadata — it's the accountability layer. Every data point traceable to a source is a data point that can be verified or contested. The CONFLICTING SOURCES flag is the CoWork model working correctly: surfaces the conflict, stops, waits for the human who can resolve it.
Next: classify which tasks should never be delegated — the non-delegation audit.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Here's what the body just demonstrated. Next: classify which tasks should never be delegated — the non-delegation audit. Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Build the Weekly Operations Packet with Claude: Capstone Demo] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
